name: Riot Platforms, Inc.

profile.description: Riot Platforms is one of the largest publicly traded Bitcoin mining companies in North America, operating large-scale mining facilities in Texas. The company is pivoting toward AI/HPC data center hosting, leveraging its massive power infrastructure (1.4+ GW capacity) to serve hyperscaler customers.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Riot posted record full-year 2025 revenue of $647.4M (up 72% YoY) and mined 5,686 bitcoin [1]. The company began generating revenue from a data center lease with AMD in January 2026, marking the first concrete step in its AI pivot. The 651M Whinstone acquisition built the Rockdale mining facility, one of the largest Bitcoin mining operations globally. AI Deployment (1/2): The AMD data center lease represents early AI infrastructure revenue, and the company is building a 112 MW data center at Corsicana for AI/HPC workloads. Board additions include former Meta and Microsoft data center executives, showing serious commitment to the AI pivot [2]. Market Share (1/2): Riot's deployed hash rate of 36.5 EH/s (up 29% YoY) makes it one of the largest Bitcoin miners globally. The 1.4+ GW power capacity positions it as a potential top-tier data center operator [1]. Pipeline (1/2): Starboard Value estimates the data center business alone could be worth $23-53/share if Riot monetizes its full capacity. The 112 MW Corsicana data center begins construction Q1 2026. However, this is potential rather than proven revenue [2]. R&D Intensity (0/1): Riot's innovation is in energy infrastructure and operations rather than R&D-intensive technology development.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): Riot's mission revolves around Bitcoin mining and energy infrastructure — a financial/technical objective without a broader social purpose. The pivot to AI data centers adds economic relevance but doesn't constitute an inspiring cause [2]. Community Impact (0/2): Greenpeace has specifically called out Riot as 'the company behind the most energy-intensive Bitcoin mine in the U.S.' U.S. Senators wrote letters concerning Riot's high energy use, air pollution, and noise pollution affecting surrounding communities. ERCOT incentive lobbying adds political controversy [2]. Employee Retention (1/2): CEO Jason Les and the founding team have maintained continuity through multiple Bitcoin cycles, and the promotion of insider Jason Chung to CFO shows internal development. However, executive compensation ($21M+ for CEO in a year with $510M losses) drew scrutiny [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): Starboard Value's 3.5% stake and activist campaign demonstrate institutional conviction. The company maintains 18,005 BTC holdings ($1.9B+ in liquidity), showing bitcoin conviction [1][2].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Riot's financial profile reflects a deeply unprofitable mining operation. D/E of 30.3% is conservative. However, ROE is negative at -22.1%, and profit margin is catastrophically negative at -102.4%, indicating the company loses more than $1 for every $1 of revenue [6]. Forward P/E is deeply negative (-42.4). Revenue grew 7.2% YoY with a 3-year CAGR of 35.7% (driven by Bitcoin price appreciation and mining expansion) [1]. The average mining cost of $49,645/BTC in 2025 (up from $32,216 in 2024) demonstrates rising cost pressure as network hash rate increases 52% annually. The forward PEG is unavailable due to negative earnings. Liquidity floor cleared: 18.7M × $14.33 = $268M daily [6].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): Bitcoin mining faces a unique AI dynamic — it's not threatened by AI, but the AI data center opportunity could transform Riot's business model entirely. The company's 1.4+ GW power infrastructure in Texas is extremely valuable for AI/HPC workloads, which is why Starboard is pushing for faster AI pivot. However, the core Bitcoin mining business is exposed to hash rate competition and energy cost pressures that are indifferent to AI trends. The physical power infrastructure (substations, transmission, cooling) creates a genuine AI-immune asset base [1][2]. AI Opportunity (1/1): Riot generates early AI revenue from its AMD data center lease (started January 2026) and is constructing a 112 MW AI/HPC data center. Starboard estimates the full 1.4 GW capacity could generate $1.6B annual EBITDA in AI/HPC — more than double current mining operations [2].

infinite_game.summary: Riot Platforms operates in a finite, zero-sum industry — Bitcoin mining is fundamentally about computing more hashes than competitors to win block rewards. The AI pivot is strategic but driven by activist pressure rather than organic purpose. The company lacks a cause beyond profit maximization.

just_cause.analysis: Riot lacks a Just Cause. Bitcoin mining is a competitive race to compute hashes — there is no vision of a better future, no service to others beyond network security, and no idealistic goal that can never be fully achieved. The AI data center pivot is economically motivated rather than cause-driven. CEO Les's background as a professional poker player frames the business as a calculated bet rather than a mission [2]. (0/3 pts)

trusting_teams.analysis: The promotion of CFO Jason Chung from within and the addition of highly relevant board members (former Meta and Microsoft data center executives) show organizational investment. The company maintains operational continuity through Bitcoin price volatility. However, the CEO compensation controversy ($21M+ in a year with $510M losses) and Starboard's activist pressure suggest governance challenges [2]. (1/2 pts)

worthy_rivals.analysis: Bitcoin mining is inherently a finite competitive game — miners compete for a fixed block reward that halves every four years. Riot's competitive language focuses on hash rate dominance, cost per BTC, and facility scale rather than learning from rivals. The Starboard letter demanding faster AI pivot treats the competition as a race to pivot [1][2]. (0/1 pt)

existential_flexibility.analysis: The pivot from pure Bitcoin mining to AI/HPC data center infrastructure represents meaningful strategic flexibility. Replacing Bitcoin mining metrics with data center metrics in executive compensation signals organizational commitment to the transformation. The AMD lease and Corsicana construction are concrete execution steps [2]. (1/2 pts)

courage_to_lead.analysis: Maintaining $1.9B in total liquidity including 18,005 BTC during market uncertainty shows conviction. The AI pivot investment (112 MW Corsicana data center) requires upfront capital commitment. However, this courage is largely forced by Starboard's activist campaign rather than organic leadership initiative [1][2]. (1/2 pts)

investment_thesis: Riot Platforms offers a unique asymmetric opportunity: the company's 1.4+ GW power infrastructure in Texas is worth significantly more as AI/HPC data center capacity than as Bitcoin mining operations. Starboard Value estimates the data center business alone could generate $1.6B annual EBITDA — worth $23-53/share vs. the current $14.33 price [2]. The AMD lease (January 2026) validates the AI pivot, record revenue of $647.4M (72% growth) shows mining scale, and $1.9B in total liquidity (including 18,005 BTC) provides financial flexibility. The company's gross margin of ~50% on mining demonstrates operational efficiency when Bitcoin prices cooperate [1].
thesis_continued: The bear case is substantial: Riot is producing Bitcoin at a loss, with mining costs of $49,645/BTC rising as network hash rate grows 52% annually — this cost structure is only viable when Bitcoin prices exceed $50,000 [1]. Profit margin of -102.4% means the company loses more than it earns. The AI pivot is promising but unproven at scale — the AMD lease generates modest revenue compared to the infrastructure's potential. Bloomberg ESG scores of 1.06/10 (0/10 on environmental) reflect the massive energy consumption controversy. Executive compensation of $21M+ in a year with $510M losses signals governance misalignment. The company is a leveraged bet on both Bitcoin price appreciation AND successful AI pivot execution — both must work for the thesis to play out [1][2].
key_catalysts: AI/HPC data center expansion — 112 MW Corsicana construction in Q1 2026 with potential to monetize full 1.4 GW capacity
Bitcoin price appreciation above $50,000 restoring mining profitability across the 36.5 EH/s deployed hash rate
Starboard Value activist campaign accelerating AI pivot timeline and unlocking infrastructure value
key_risks: Bitcoin mining cost of $49,645/BTC (rising annually) means mining is unprofitable at current prices, creating cash burn
AI data center pivot unproven at scale — revenue from AMD lease is modest vs. $647M mining revenue
Environmental and governance ESG scores (1.06/10 Bloomberg) and $21M+ CEO compensation in loss year limit institutional interest
